title: Testing Your SSO Integration
heading: Testing Your SSO Integration
content:
/\*! tailwindcss v4.1.17 | MIT License | https://tailwindcss.com \*/ @layer theme, base, components, utilities; @layer utilities;

> ❗️
> 
> ### 
> 
> Clever Complete Agreement Required
> 
> [](#clever-complete-agreement-required)
> 
> This feature is included with a Clever Complete subscription. Sign up [here](https://www.clever.com/developer-signup) or email your Application Success Manager to learn more.

Use this page to test your Clever SSO integration before certification and make sure each supported login path works as expected.

## 

Before you start

[](#before-you-start)

Before you begin testing, confirm these three things:

1.  **Which integration type are you testing?**  
    Keep Clever Single Sign-On and Clever Library SSO separate while testing. The flows are similar, but the supported users, scopes, and certification expectations are different.
    
2.  **Which test users should you use?**  
    In most cases, you should use the default sandbox users associated with the integration type you are testing.
    
3.  **Which scopes do you expect to have?**  
    Data access changes depending on whether you are testing Clever Single Sign-On, Clever Library, or an account that also has Secure Sync access.
    

If you need a refresher on integration differences, see [Clever Complete Integration Types and Quickstart Guides](https://dev.clever.com/docs/integration-types).

## 

Required settings

[](#required-settings)

Before you test, make sure these settings are configured in your developer dashboard:

**Settings > Integration**

![](https://files.readme.io/ee56ca0721661aa76aa3ca10c2fe8290aedd035535223bf7ff3111614b4d2551-image.png)

### 

Supported user types

[](#supported-user-types)

This setting controls which user types can use your SSO integration.

Clever Library only supports **teachers** and **students**.

### 

Redirect URI

[](#redirect-uri)

The redirect URI tells Clever where to return the authorization code or access token after a user authorizes your application.

This setting is an allowlist of redirect URIs that Clever accepts. If your authentication request uses a redirect URI that is not listed here, Clever returns an error.

If no redirect URI is specified in an authentication request, Clever uses the first redirect URI in the list.

> 🚧
> 
> ### 
> 
> **Your default redirect URI must be publicly accessible for certification**
> 
> [](#your-default-redirect-uri-must-be-publicly-accessible-for-certification)
> 
> If your first redirect URI is not publicly accessible, Clever will not be able to certify your integration.

## 

Test users and test plan

[](#test-users-and-test-plan)

Your developer account includes default test users that you can use to validate SSO behavior.

> 📘
> 
> ### 
> 
> **Use the SSO Test Plan**
> 
> [](#use-the-sso-test-plan)
> 
> If you want a faster path through the expected test cases, use the [SSO Test Plan](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1VTFeG9J9zvYNgyukEEW7iuNC_sv65pLoyxHa34DQgks/edit?gid=0#gid=0).

Pay close attention to which user type and integration type you are testing so you do not accidentally validate the wrong path.

## 

Understand the SSO entry points

[](#understand-the-sso-entry-points)

Users can start Clever SSO in multiple ways. Your testing should cover the entry points your integration supports.

For a broader overview, see [Clever Single Sign-On - Overview](https://dev.clever.com/docs/getting-started-with-clever-sso).

If your integration requires a `state` parameter, review [Best Practices & Edge Cases](https://dev.clever.com/docs/best-practices-edge-cases#state-parameters) before testing Clever-initiated logins.

> ❗️
> 
> ### 
> 
> **Support Clever Portal login**
> 
> [](#support-clever-portal-login)
> 
> Most Clever users launch applications from the Clever Portal. Clever strongly recommends supporting Clever Portal login.
> 
> Log in with Clever on your own site is optional.

> 💪
> 
> ### 
> 
> **Pro tip**
> 
> [](#pro-tip)
> 
> If your browser does not already have district context, you may see the Clever school picker.
> 
> You will not be able to find your sandbox district by searching for school names. Use your sandbox district ID instead.
> 
> To skip the school picker during testing, add `district_id` to your authorization URL:
> 
> `https://clever.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&redirect_uri=&client_id=&district_id=`
> 
> ![](https://files.readme.io/b633bfaba4a00b35c4e5600930ec050e5cb7f7309222dd3c0c034a02f656bc00-image.png)

> 🚧
> 
> ### 
> 
> **Testing is part of certification**
> 
> [](#testing-is-part-of-certification)
> 
> If you need a refresher on certification requirements, see [Certification Overview](https://dev.clever.com/docs/certification-overview).

## 

Ways to test

[](#ways-to-test)

### 

SSO Test Plan

[](#sso-test-plan)

The [SSO Test Plan](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1VTFeG9J9zvYNgyukEEW7iuNC_sv65pLoyxHa34DQgks/edit?usp=sharing) is the recommended way to validate your integration before certification.

Default sandbox credentials commonly look like this:

-   **Student:** username and password are the student’s `student_number`
-   **Teacher:** username and password are the teacher’s `teacher_number`
-   **School admin:** username and password are the school admin’s `staff_id`

If you see the **School Picker** while testing and your browser does not have district context cached, use your **sandbox district ID** to locate the district.

![](https://files.readme.io/cb0032824305b8da1cca72bb981e7a0ac962afa535a73c750fc3e0bd238eb3be-image.png)

You can find your sandbox district ID in your dashboard.

![](https://files.readme.io/cef1b6515a8725b2fbe7fa7c889adcc855bf343b70a8d8511361afde0e962ea8-image.png)

### 

Developer dashboard shortcut

[](#developer-dashboard-shortcut)

In a development account, you can type a test user’s name while viewing a district. After you select the user, the dashboard shows a **Log in as...** button in the top-right corner.
